entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal grain crop used for food and fodder.

entities.name: HYBRID SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum produced by crossing two different parent lines to achieve heterosis.

entities.name: YIELD ADVANTAGE
entities.description: The increase in productivity compared to non-hybrid or traditional varieties.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental condition characterized by a lack of water available for plant growth.

entities.name: SEMIARID AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low and irregular rainfall.

entities.name: NIGERIAN GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources of sorghum originating from Nigeria.

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: The country and environment for which the hybrid sorghum is being developed.

entities.name: MALE STERILITY MAINTAINERS
entities.description: Lines used to maintain the genetic purity of male-sterile female parents in hybrid seed production.

entities.name: MALE STERILITY MAINTENANCE
entities.description: The ability to maintain male sterility in female parent lines.

entities.name: FEMALE PARENTS
entities.description: The male-sterile lines used as the female component in hybrid crossing.

entities.name: SORGHUM LANDRACES
entities.description: Local varieties of sorghum adapted to specific environments.

entities.name: SEED PARENTS
entities.description: The parental lines used for the production of hybrid seeds.
